meta.description: Select predictive biomarker features using elastic-net penalized regression (glmnet). Inputs: feature matrix, response variable. Outputs: selected feature list, regularization path, coefficient plot, cross-validated lambda and performance metrics.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated identifiers, study results, or numeric claims were observed across the seven executions.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed within computational feature-selection scope and did not provide diagnostic or treatment advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow consistently enforced binary classification boundaries and used a method appropriate for the documented task.

code_usability.detail: The R CLI executed successfully in valid scenarios, passed the bundled test suite, and rejected invalid inputs with structured errors.

functional_suitability.note: Functional suitability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

reliability.note: Reliability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

human_usability.note: Strict CSV expectations reduce forgiveness for near-miss user inputs.

security.note: Security was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

maintainability.note: Maintainability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

agent_specific.note: Strong scope fences and reproducibility controls; execution mode could be surfaced earlier.

key_strengths: The skill contract is tightly aligned with the implementation, including arguments, outputs, and structured SKILL_* failures.
The R implementation is modular, reproducible, and test-backed, with seed control, timeout handling, warning capture, and session-info export.
Scope enforcement is strong: multiclass and invalid-parameter inputs fail early and clearly instead of silently degrading.
Ridge-mode behavior is documented and implemented correctly, preventing dense coefficients from being mislabeled as sparse selected features.

recommendations.title: Add compact run summary artifact
recommendations.problem: Several successful runs produced a header-only selected_features.csv. Users must inspect logs or coefficient tables to know whether this came from ridge selection or from no coefficient passing the threshold.
recommendations.root_cause: The workflow exports the raw analysis tables but not a compact metadata artifact that explains the run outcome.
recommendations.fix: Write a small run_summary.csv or run_summary.json containing selected alpha, lambda rule, effective nfolds, selected-feature count, and the zero-selection reason. Add this file to the documented outputs table.

recommendations.title: Document non-ridge zero-selection outcome
recommendations.problem: A valid fixed-alpha run completed with no selected features at the chosen lambda, but the current documentation emphasizes the ridge case more than the general zero-selection case.
recommendations.root_cause: The docs describe empty selected_features.csv mainly as a ridge behavior, which leaves one successful non-ridge outcome under-explained.
recommendations.fix: Add one explicit example and one troubleshooting note showing a non-ridge run with zero selected features, and tell users to inspect model_coefficients.csv plus the warning message.

recommendations.title: Surface R CLI mode earlier
recommendations.problem: The workflow is clearly an R CLI skill in practice, but that contract only becomes obvious after reading deeper usage sections.
recommendations.root_cause: Frontmatter and the high-level description focus on the analysis task, while execution-mode information appears later in the document.
recommendations.fix: Add a short early line such as 'Execution mode: R CLI (Rscript scripts/main.R)' while keeping the existing detailed usage examples unchanged.
